title: Ensure the principle of least privilege is used when assigning  roles to a Managed Identity

input_row.title: Ensure the principle of least privilege is used when assigning  roles to a Managed Identity
input_row.description: When using either a user-assigned or system-assigned managed identity, those identities may require a role or privilege assignment to perform a desired function. The roles or privileges assigned to that identity should be assigned with the principle of least privilege in mind - the identity is given the minimum levels of access or permissions needed to perform the job.
input_row.rationale: Threat actors may attempt to compromise service accounts as anomalous activity on these accounts can sometimes be more challenging to detect. Limiting the permissions or roles available to a managed identity or service account assists in mitigating the systemic exploitation that a service account can perform if compromised.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal For each Container Instance that uses an identity or credential: 1. Open the Container Instances blade. 2. Select a named container instance. 3. Click on Identity under the Settings section. 4. Review the System Assigned and User Assigned tabs for assigned identities. For a System Assigned identity, click on Azure role assignments and review the assigned roles for appropriate restriction. For User assigned identities, click on the name of each User assigned managed identity, then click on Azure role assignments in the left panel to review assigned roles for appropriate restriction.
input_row.remediation: NOTE: Remediation will vary based on the needs of your environment. Before remediating, determine the scope and requirements of the Role Assignments necessary for your environment: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active- directory/roles/permissions-reference Remediate from Azure Portal For each Container Instance that uses an identity or credential: 1. Open the Container Instances blade. 2. Select a named container instance. 3. Click on Identity under the Settings section. 4. Review the System Assigned and User Assigned tabs for assigned identities. For a System Assigned identity, click on Azure role assignments and Add or Remove assigned roles for appropriate restriction. For User assigned identities, click on the name of each User assigned managed identity, then click on Azure role assignments in the left panel to Add or Remove assigned roles for appropriate restriction.
input_row.impact: All service accounts should be inventoried and reviewed from time to time for necessity and role or privilege assignment.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**  
- **Automated:** The audit control regarding the principle of least privilege for Managed Identities can be verified using Azure APIs and CLI commands. The rationale stresses the importance of limiting permissions, which aligns well with common checks available through Azure's management tools.

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_container_instance_managed_identity_least_privilege_roles

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**  
- Use Azure SDK's `ManagedIdentityClient` (from the `azure.mgmt.managedidentity` library) to query role assignments for Managed Identities.
- Use Azure CLI commands:
  - `az container show --name <container_name> --resource-group <resource_group>` to retrieve details of the container instance, including its identity.
  - `az role assignment list --assignee <managed_identity_id>` to list the roles assigned to the specific Managed Identity.
- Specific resources to enumerate include container instances in the specified resource group.
- The check will validate that the assigned roles to the Managed Identity adhere to the principle of least privilege.
- Clear Pass/Fail criteria:
  - For pass, the roles assigned must meet the least privilege standard; for example, not having roles like "Owner" or "Contributor" unless absolutely necessary.
- To confirm compliance, validate that any Managed Identity does not have excessive permissions assigned beyond what is required for specific tasks as defined by the application requirements.

This ensures that the audit process systematically determines compliance and validates the security posture of Managed Identities used within Azure Container Instances.
